spec_section: Attribution Merkle tree, domain-separated construction (Day-145 audit): leaf = sha256(0x00 || leaf_hex_utf8), internal = sha256(0x01 || left_hex_utf8 || right_hex_utf8), leaves sorted ascending by code point before hashing, odd trailing node promoted unchanged, empty set = sha256("empty")

vectors.name: parity-1-leaves
vectors.description: 1-leaf multiset; leaves are sha256 of the listed leaf_inputs; expected_root is the domain-separated attribution Merkle root shared by the TypeScript, Go, and Python SDKs

vectors.name: parity-2-leaves
vectors.description: 2-leaf multiset; leaves are sha256 of the listed leaf_inputs; expected_root is the domain-separated attribution Merkle root shared by the TypeScript, Go, and Python SDKs

vectors.name: parity-3-leaves
vectors.description: 3-leaf multiset; leaves are sha256 of the listed leaf_inputs; expected_root is the domain-separated attribution Merkle root shared by the TypeScript, Go, and Python SDKs

vectors.name: parity-5-leaves
vectors.description: 5-leaf multiset; leaves are sha256 of the listed leaf_inputs; expected_root is the domain-separated attribution Merkle root shared by the TypeScript, Go, and Python SDKs

vectors.name: parity-8-leaves
vectors.description: 8-leaf multiset; leaves are sha256 of the listed leaf_inputs; expected_root is the domain-separated attribution Merkle root shared by the TypeScript, Go, and Python SDKs

vectors.name: collision-negative-3-vs-4-duplicate
vectors.description: Odd-node duplication regression (CVE-2012-2459 class): the 4-leaf multiset that duplicates the last leaf of the 3-leaf set must fold to a DIFFERENT root; both roots are pinned and the runner recomputes and compares them
